Multicultural Team Development: Built high-performing, diverse teams focused on service innovation. Career Highlights:     Financial Turnaround at Soneva Kiri: Boosted GOP margin by 15% by implementing targeted cost control measures and streamlining operational efficiencies across departments. Championed Award-Winning Environmental Initiatives: At Six Senses Con Dao, spearheaded impactful sustainability programs, including a successful turtle hatching initiative, energy conservation strategies, and the launch of long-term environmental projects. Ammarawadee Cheowit  – GM at NH Boat Lagoon Phuket Resort, Minor Hotels (5 Stars)    Ammarawadee is a seasoned professional with over 15 years of leadership experience in luxury resorts and 4–5 star hotels across Thailand’s top tourist destinations, including Koh Samui, Koh Phangan, Phuket, and Hua Hin–Cha-Am. Her career reflects a strong trajectory of growth, innovation, and operational excellence, with a proven ability to drive commercial success, elevate service standards, and lead high-performing teams.  She holds a Bachelor’s degree in Economics from Thammasat University, an MBA in Marketing from Ramkhamhang University, and a Micro MBA in Marketing from Chulalongkorn University—demonstrating a solid academic foundation in both business and strategic marketing.  Current Role & Motivation for Career Move:  Position: General Manager, NH Boat Lagoon Phuket Resort (Minor Hotels) Property Scale: 271 Rooms Scope of Responsibility: Full-spectrum leadership across Finance, HR, Sales & Marketing, and E-commerce. Supported by a Hotel Manager for F&B, Front Office, and Housekeeping operations. Reason for Exploring: Seeking career advancement and new challenges to further excel in the hospitality industry and position herself among top-tier executives.  Strengths:     Commercial Acumen & Strategic Growth: Deep expertise in budget forecasting, expense control, and revenue optimization across multiple channels including OTAs, wholesale, and direct bookings. Successfully repositioned market strategy in response to changing tourism trends (e.g., pivoting from Chinese to Indian and Turkish markets in 2025), exceeding budget targets in Q1.  Market Insight & Brand Alignment: Strong analytical skills in customer segmentation and market trends, aligning service offerings with brand positioning. Led rebranding initiatives and ensured compliance with brand standards, including renovation oversight and safety compliance.   Operational Excellence: Demonstrated success in pre-opening and renovation phases, with strategic planning focused on fixed expense analysis and segmentation to achieve GOP targets. Proven ability to maintain operational continuity and revenue generation even during partial inventory closures.  Leadership & Team Development: Built high-performing teams through training programs, service standard development, and hands-on leadership. Recognized for elevating service quality and guest satisfaction across multiple properties.     Career Highlights:     Transformed Commercial Performance at NH Boat Lagoon Phuket Resort: Achieved a remarkable 100% increase in GOP year-over-year by redefining pricing strategies & optimizing revenue. Successfully doubled all key performance indicators (KPIs) from 2023 on the first NH-branded property under Minor Hotels in Southeast Asia.  Award-Winning Operational Excellence: Led the improvement of hotel standards at Springfield@Sea Resort & Spa to winning the Tourism Award #13 in 2021. Introduced innovative amenities including a water park and a beachfront bar concept, which boosted guest engagement and the hotel’s revenue.  Areas to Watch Out:     Limited exposure to international chains: While she has successfully led the first NH brand hotel in Southeast Asia, broader experience with global chains could further enhance her profile.  Frequent job changes: Her dynamic career path reflects a strong drive for growth and challenge, but may raise concerns about long-term stability.  Highly proactive personality: Her energetic and challenge-seeking nature is a strength, but may benefit from strategic pacing in career planning.  Compensation Package:  Base: 260,000 THB  Housing allowance: 70,000 THB Gas allowance: 6,000 THB Fixed Stock: 5% from salary +on top 5% Bonus: 40% of annual salary if achieve KPI (Owner not approved) Notice Period: 2 months Expectation package: at least 300,000 THB base

Nationality : British - Thai Current Role: Chief Marketing Officer – Com Seven (Studio7, Banana IT, Fintech & lifestyle sub-brands) Experience: GM – L’Oréal Luxe (Giorgio Armani & Biotherm), CMO – McDonald’s Thailand, Marketing Director – KFC Thailand (Yum), Global Brand & Innovation Roles – BAT London, Unilever UK/Thailand  •	Sonthaya is a senior marketing and brand leader with 22+ years across global FMCG and large-scale retail, combining deep global brand-building rigor (Unilever, BAT) with hands-on, full P&L omnichannel leadership in Thailand (L’Oréal, McDonald’s, KFC, Com7). She brings strong capability in brand strategy, digital/e-commerce acceleration, CRM/lifecycle, and integrated marketing execution. •	In her current CMO role at Com7 (since Sep 2024), she leads a 75-person marketing organization and owns 360° portfolio brand strategy. She has pushed digital-first and Gen Z engagement (TikTok ecosystem, LIVE commerce, in-store “TikTok Troops”), while managing partner alignment at scale (notably Apple). She frames AI/automation as a practical enabler for segmentation, content efficiency, and O2O conversion rather than a standalone KPI — aligned with CHR’s “AI as a plus” reality. •	Previously at L’Oréal Thailand (2019–2024), she served as GM for two luxury brands with full P&L, leading omnichannel teams across offline, marketplace, and brand sites. She delivered outsized growth via advocacy models, category pivots, and strong digital platform launches, showing she can reposition brands and improve commercial outcomes simultaneously. Her earlier CMO/Marketing Director roles at McDonald’s and KFC demonstrate turnaround experience in high-pressure consumer businesses, with sharp discipline on insight → positioning → execution. •	A key driver behind her move into Thai retail was to deepen entrepreneurial, fast-cycle execution. She has seen both sides: global governance discipline and local “speed + opportunism.” Importantly for CHR, she is candid about operating in layered, traditional environments and has direct experience pushing transformation in legacy mindsets — a close parallel to CHR’s current state.  Key Strengths •	Global-standard brand strategy + positioning, with proven local execution in Thailand •	Strong omnichannel leadership: digital, e-commerce, social, PR, CRM, advocacy, in-store activation •	Full P&L ownership and commercial literacy while staying brand-led (L’Oréal, McDonald’s, Com7) •	Transformation and change leadership in traditional/legacy cultures; practical AI/automation adoption •	Senior people leader (75-person org) with structured KPI discipline + creative thinking •	Strong stakeholder management across HQ/global standards, partners (esp. Apple), and Thai-style leadership layers  Areas to Watch Out •	No direct hospitality background; will need a learning curve on hotel guest-journey nuances (though her luxury retail + food turnaround experience is adjacent and likely transferable). •	Used to organizations where marketing holds strong decision rights; CHR’s more siloed/hierarchical setup may require deliberate expectation-setting on influence and speed of change. •	Performance/CRM strength is high, but her most recent context is retail-conversion heavy — ensure alignment that CHR’s priority is brand perception + demand generation, not purely sales KPIs.  Compensation •	Basic Salary : THB 500,000/ month •	Telephone Allowance : THB 1,000/ month •	Gasoline Allowance : THB 5,000/ month •	Performance Bonus : 4 months •	Notice Period :  1 month

Overall, Anthony comes across as a strong and credible luxury hospitality candidate with solid international exposure across Four Seasons, Ritz-Carlton, Kempinski, and Shangri-La. He is currently Resort Manager at Four Seasons Chiang Mai and appears to be operating close to GM level in his current role, with broad responsibility across hotel operations and regular involvement in owner reviews, budgeting, forecasting, and commercial discussions.  From a profile standpoint, his key strengths are: •	Strong luxury pedigree with long-term experience in top-tier brands •	Deep F&B background, which is highly relevant for this role •	Good resort operations exposure and people leadership maturity •	Strong guest-facing mindset and natural focus on personalization and service recovery •	Comfortable in stakeholder and ownership environments •	Clear ambition and readiness to step up into a first GM role  He also presented himself well in the interview — polished, thoughtful, and commercially aware. He speaks with confidence and seems to have the right personality for a luxury environment where visible leadership and guest connection matter. Based on our discussion, I believe the Reserve style of leadership is something he understands and would likely enjoy.  At the same time, there are a few areas to further test in the next round: •	This would be his first GM role, so full end-to-end GM accountability still needs to be validated •	His background is F&B-led, although he has made deliberate effort to broaden himself operationally and commercially •	He has not been to Krabi yet, so destination knowledge and market positioning should be probed further •	Family and schooling logistics will be an important practical consideration, although he appears open to relocation  On motivation, he was transparent that Four Seasons is discussing future progression with him, but nothing is confirmed. He is therefore open to strong opportunities externally, especially if they allow him to stay in Thailand and move into a true GM role. He showed genuine interest in the Reserve Krabi opportunity and asked relevant questions around concept, positioning, and vision.  For current package, he shared the following: •	Net salary: USD 8,500 per month •	Bonus: approximately 25–30% annually •	Notice period: 2 months •	Schooling support currently provided for his son •	International insurance provided  His expectation for a first-time GM role is around NET USD 10,000–11,000 per month for base salary.  My overall view is that Anthony is a strong candidate to progress to the next stage. He is not a “finished” experienced GM profile, but he is a credible step-up GM candidate with strong luxury foundations, especially for a property where F&B, guest engagement, and brand presence are critical.

I had a discussion with Claudio Roscigno, Senior Director, HWS Middle East & Africa and ASO EMEA Development at Hilton, and found his profile quite interesting, so wanted to share a quick overview and initial perspective for your consideration.  Claudio is currently leading commercial operations across Middle East, Africa, and Türkiye, covering ~320 hotels and ~USD 500M+ revenue. His background spans across sales, marketing, and revenue, with prior experience at ONYX (Bangkok), Caesars, Rosewood, Four Seasons, and IHG.  From the conversation, he comes across as someone who naturally operates as a builder, with a strong focus on bringing marketing, sales, and revenue together rather than running them separately. His marketing exposure is quite practical — especially around destination campaigns, partnerships, and loyalty — and he consistently links this back to demand generation and revenue, rather than brand in isolation.  On leadership, he feels more balanced and people-aware. His style is less directive and more about guiding, challenging thinking, and building alignment. He places a lot of emphasis on trust and team engagement, while still being clear that performance needs to be addressed when required. In that sense, his approach is quite different from the challenges we’ve seen previously.  At the same time, he is clearly a hands-on and visible leader — close to the team, close to customers, and involved in the detail when needed. Neutral : It is important to note that the Group may not necessarily require a high-profile CFO role at the CG level. At the same time, there could be a positive role for him if positioned as a senior strategic support to K. Tos, particularly in relation to European activities. In that capacity, he could contribute analytical thinking, financial discipline, and structured evaluation of projects while helping assess opportunities, partnerships, and investment decisions. This type of role could allow the Group to benefit from his strategic and financial perspective without necessarily placing him in a traditional CFO leadership at CG level.

Executive Summary  Vo Tuan Sy is a senior Vietnamese finance executive with over 24 years of experience across FMCG, consumer healthcare, industrial, manufacturing, and large local group environments. His background combines CFO-level leadership, commercial finance, controllership, transformation, and group-level performance management. His CV reflects CFO / Finance Director experience with Beiersdorf Vietnam, Cummins DKSH, Masan Group, Mondelez, Marico, L’Oréal, BAT, Unilever, and Holcim.  During the interview, he demonstrates strong commercial finance orientation, a hands-on business partnering style, and a clear ability to use finance as a driver of performance rather than only as a control function. He appears particularly strong in connecting finance with commercial teams, using data to influence decisions, simplifying processes, and building team capability.  Personal & Family Information  Based in Ho Chi Minh City. Married; his wife also works in finance in a more junior-level role. Has 3 children: Eldest around 19–20, entering university. 1 child in high school. 1 child in primary school.  He mentioned that his family situation is now stable.  He stated that he is mobile and open to working in another country if required.  Reason for leaving Masan was linked to family responsibility, as his mother was unwell and he needed to reduce weekend intensity at that time.   Current Role & Motivation He is currently CFO / Finance & IT Director at Beiersdorf Vietnam, reporting to both the Country CEO and the Regional Finance Director. He leads a lean Finance and IT team of around 13 people, with 6 direct reports. During the interview, he explained that he is now open to a bigger platform and larger business scale. His motivation is to create greater impact as CFO, build finance as a strong business support function, and drive high performance across the whole organization. He was informed that the discussion is part of succession planning and future pipeline development, not a confirmed live vacancy.  Key Experience Highlights 1. Commercial Finance & Business Partnering During the interview, he demonstrates strong ability to work closely with commercial teams. He described how he partners with sales and marketing leaders to align channel targets, profitability, and productivity before CEO-level reviews.  He uses data such as: Sales by channel Profitability by channel Revenue per headcount Sales productivity Store visit productivity External benchmarks  This indicates a practical, data-led approach to influencing business leaders.  2. Performance Turnaround & EBIT Improvement He shared that when he joined his current company, the business was loss-making. He stated that he helped turn the company profitable in the first year and subsequently supported annual profit growth of around 20–25%. His CV also highlights double-digit sales CAGR and strong EBIT expansion over multiple years.   During the interview, he attributed the results to: Building strong sales controlling capability Simplifying finance processes Supporting commercial teams rather than blocking them Motivating teams to win by customer and product category Driving accountability through data and regular performance dialogue   3. Transformation & Automation During the interview, he gave a practical example of implementing Power BI reporting. Although SAP and BI systems already existed globally, Vietnam’s business needs were more specific and could not be fully addressed by standard reports.  He: Identified the workload issue caused by Excel-based reporting. Engaged an external IT company to train the team. Initially outsourced report building for learning purposes. Then trained internal finance and sales operations teams to build reports themselves. Now has around 10 key Power BI reports used by commercial teams and the wider organization.  This suggests a pragmatic transformation style: start with capability building, simplify work, then scale adoption.  4. Group-Level and Local Vietnamese Business Exposure His Masan Group experience is relevant. During the interview, he described working at group level, supporting subsidiaries and affiliates, reporting to the Board, and intervening directly when affiliate companies had performance issues.  He also understands the difference between MNC and local Vietnamese corporate environments: MNCs: more structured, value-driven, process-oriented. Local large groups: more entrepreneurial, intense, target-driven, and experimental. This is useful for assessing his potential fit with Central Group, particularly given the mix of professional management, family ownership, and local market realities.  Strengths 1. Strong Commercial Finance Mindset He does not position finance as only accounting, reporting, or governance. During the interview, he demonstrates a clear orientation toward revenue growth, profitability, channel productivity, and commercial decision support. 2. Data-Led Influencing Style He appears to influence through facts and structured analysis rather than authority. He uses internal and external benchmarks to create alignment and challenge business assumptions. 3. Practical Transformation Capability His Power BI example was credible and hands-on. He focuses not only on system implementation but also on team learning and adoption. 4. People Development Orientation His CV states that he developed and promoted around 30% of finance team members into higher roles.  During the interview, he also spoke about coaching both finance and commercial leaders, including helping a senior sales leader realign with company values and performance expectations. 5. Relevant Vietnam Market Exposure He has deep Vietnam experience across FMCG, manufacturing, consumer healthcare, and local group environments. He also has supplier-side exposure to modern trade and Central / Big C through previous commercial finance roles. 6. Positive Attitude Toward Thai Culture He expressed positive views about working with Thai colleagues and said he enjoys Thai working culture, describing it as humble, helpful, reasonable, and supportive.  Areas to Watch Out 1. Direct Retail CFO Experience He has strong exposure to modern trade from the supplier side, but he has not yet operated as CFO inside a large retail platform. Pascal may need to test his understanding of retail economics, store operations, omnichannel complexity, inventory, shrinkage, trading terms, logistics, and multi-format retail P&L. 2. Scale of Current Team His current direct organization is relatively lean, with around 13 Finance and IT team members. Central Retail Vietnam may require broader leadership across a larger, more complex finance organization. 3.
